name: Recipe 4 — Atomic handoff (claim, synthetic)
comment: Alice yields recipe_handoff_yield with 0.01 NEAR attached, nominating Bob as recipient. The contract holds the deposit and schedules a callback (from=alice, to=bob, amount=0.01 NEAR). A resume tx fires recipe_handoff_resume (permissionless — anyone can pull the trigger, and the funds still go to the stored `to`); the callback fires (Ok arm) and transfers the funds to Bob via Promise::new(to).transfer(amount). The single receipt scheduled at yield time carried the transfer outcome all along — resume just delivered the signal to unblock it. No escrow table, no refund method, no polling.

events.title: Recipe 4 — atomic handoff
events.body: alice attaches 0.01 NEAR to a yield nominating bob. the contract holds the deposit and schedules a callback. on bob's resume, the callback transfers the funds to bob atomically. one receipt, two possible endings.

events.title: tx1 — alice yields with 0.01 NEAR attached
events.body: recipe_handoff_yield(name, to=bob). the contract receives the deposit and calls Promise::new_yield("on_handoff_resumed", {from, to, amount}). the callback receipt is now scheduled — waiting for input.

events.title: tx2 — resume triggers transfer
events.body: alice signs recipe_handoff_resume(name). the waiting callback's Ok arm runs Promise::new(bob).transfer(amount) — bob is the nominated recipient stored at yield time, so the funds go to him regardless of who pulled the trigger.

events.title: resolved — 0.01 NEAR delivered to bob
events.body: trace events handoff_released and recipe_resolved_ok emitted. the transfer receipt was part of the callback's outcome, all inside the yield tx's DAG. bob's balance increased; alice's decreased. no escrow table was ever written.
